meta.description: General (fallback) discriminators used when no ESS-specific flowchart matches the patient's chief complaint. Evaluated top-down; first match determines triage level.
meta.usage: Apply these discriminators when the ESS category is unknown, unmapped, or does not have a dedicated flowchart in flowcharts.json.

general_discriminators.name: Airway threat
general_discriminators.rationale: Any compromise to the airway is immediately life-threatening.

general_discriminators.name: Inadequate breathing
general_discriminators.rationale: Respiratory rate outside survivable range without intervention.

general_discriminators.name: Circulatory compromise — shock
general_discriminators.rationale: Systolic BP <70 indicates decompensated shock requiring immediate intervention.

general_discriminators.name: Unresponsive
general_discriminators.rationale: Unresponsive patient requires immediate assessment and airway management.

general_discriminators.name: Critical oxygen desaturation
general_discriminators.rationale: SpO2 <85% indicates severe hypoxaemia requiring immediate oxygen therapy.

general_discriminators.name: Altered consciousness
general_discriminators.rationale: Responds only to pain or voice — significant neurological compromise.

general_discriminators.name: Very hot adult (>=41C)
general_discriminators.rationale: Hyperpyrexia (>=41C) risks end-organ damage and requires active cooling.

general_discriminators.name: Severe tachycardia
general_discriminators.rationale: HR >150 may indicate haemodynamic instability or a dangerous arrhythmia.

general_discriminators.name: Severe hypertension with symptoms
general_discriminators.rationale: SBP >200 with any symptoms suggests hypertensive emergency.

general_discriminators.name: Moderately febrile (>=38.5C)
general_discriminators.rationale: Fever >=38.5C warrants timely assessment but is not immediately life-threatening.

general_discriminators.name: Moderate pain — distress keywords
general_discriminators.rationale: Significant pain or distress requires timely analgesia and assessment.

general_discriminators.name: Mild tachycardia
general_discriminators.rationale: Tachycardia >110 is abnormal and may indicate underlying pathology.

general_discriminators.name: Hypothermia
general_discriminators.rationale: Core temperature <35C requires warming and investigation of cause.

general_discriminators.name: Standard presentation — no urgent discriminator matched
general_discriminators.rationale: Patient is alert, vitals within normal range, no high-acuity discriminators triggered. Standard assessment within 2 hours.
